title: Select from non-array BASICDTYPE 'bit'
body:
OS:
```
MacOS Ventura 13.3
```

Verilator Version:
```
$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.008 2023-03-04 rev UNKNOWN.REV
```

Code:
```Verilog
module Test(CLK, XOUT);
  input logic CLK;
  inout logic [15: 0] XOUT[8][8];
  always @(posedge CLK)
  begin
    XOUT[1][3] = XOUT[0][5] + 1;
  end
endmodule // Test
```

Error:
```Shell
$ verilator -sv --lint-only -Mdir /tmp /tmp/test.sv
%Error: /tmp/test.sv:6:9: Select from non-array BASICDTYPE 'bit'
                      : ... In instance Test
    6 |     XOUT[1][3] = XOUT[0][5] + 1;
      |         ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

Even a simple assignment of a constant generates the same error (the RHS does not influence the error).
I have tested the above with *Vivado* and *Quartus*, and they seem OK with that syntax.
